Title: ScRNA-seq reveals dark- and light-induced differentially expressed gene atlases of seedling leaves in Arachis hypogaea L.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes epidermal cell enlargement and leaf expansion
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dark versus light conditions; ectopic overexpression in Arabidopsis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes leaf epidermal cell enlargement and increases IAA, CTK, and ACC levels, enhancing leaf expansion
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AT-hook motif nuclear-localized protein; DUF296-containing AHL family
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed in leaves, roots, stems, pegs, seeds and pods, especially leaves, pegs and seeds; peaks in epidermal cells of etiolated seedlings
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: scRNA-seq differential expression; subcellular localization in Arabidopsis protoplasts; ectopic overexpression in Arabidopsis; SEM epidermal cell measurement; LC-MS phytohormone profiling; qPCR; DAP-seq
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: scRNA-seq, bulk RNA-seq, DAP-seq; CNCB PRJCA010983; PRJCA015968
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: classified as Common_Gene because it is a regulatory AT-hook protein controlling phytohormone-associated growth but not a canonical pathway enzyme; paper emphasizes epidermal cell enlargement and hormone-associated downstream genes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AHL17 was identified as a key dark-enriched candidate from peanut single-cell TF atlases, especially in epidermal cells. 2. Peanut AHL17 is nuclear localized and ectopic overexpression in Arabidopsis enlarged epidermal cells and leaves. 3. AHL17-OE plants accumulated higher IAA, CTK and ACC, supporting a causal link to hormone-associated growth promotion.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful candidate for engineering leaf growth vigor and possibly biomass through epidermal cell expansion, but deployment should consider pleiotropic hormone effects and early flowering
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: early flowering in Arabidopsis overexpression lines; possible pleiotropic hormone-mediated effects on development

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reduces protochlorophyllide to chlorophyllide in the chlorophyll biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as the critical restricting enzyme for chlorophyllide synthesis in this study
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: mesophyll cells; also transcript signal detected in epidermis cluster 0
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dark versus light conditions in seedling leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: dark-associated PORA abundance correlates with restricted chlorophyllide synthesis and etiolated phenotype; light transition reduces PORA transcript abundance during greening
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high transcript abundance in dark mesophyll clusters; down-regulated along dark-to-light developmental trajectory
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: scRNA-seq expression mapping in mesophyll clusters; pseudo-time trajectory analysis
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: 11 TFs with similar expression tendency in dark mesophyll cells
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: paper states two PORA genes from two sub-genomes but specific locus IDs were not given; authors propose high PORA transcript abundance under dark restricts pchlide conversion until light exposure
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PORA is a central chlorophyllide synthesis-related gene enriched in dark mesophyll cells. 2. PORA transcript abundance decreases as dark cells convert toward light-cell states. 3. The study proposes PORA as the main enzyme associated with dark-repressed chlorophyllide synthesis and leaf greening recovery upon light exposure.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate marker or engineering target for controlling greening dynamics and chlorophyll accumulation in peanut seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: altering PORA may affect etiolation/de-etiolation dynamics and photosynthetic establishment

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reduces the 8-vinyl group of divinyl chlorophyllide a in chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not dominant; similar pattern to PORA but not the major determinant according to authors
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dark versus light conditions in seedling leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: dark-associated DVR transcript pattern is similar to PORA and is proposed to contribute to restricted chlorophyllide synthesis under dark
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: similar developmental expression trend as PORA in mesophyll trajectory
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: scRNA-seq expression mapping in mesophyll cells; pseudo-time trajectory analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: authors explicitly state DVR showed a similar result to PORA but did not dominate dark-cell differentiation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DVR is another chlorophyll synthesis-related enzyme showing dark-associated expression in mesophyll cells. 2. Its trajectory pattern resembles PORA. 3. DVR contributes to dark-repressed chlorophyllide synthesis but is not considered the dominant regulator in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: possible auxiliary target for manipulating chlorophyll biosynthesis together with PORA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: may alter greening and photosynthetic establishment if perturbed
